vol_description:
This volume contains documentation for the Comprehensive Assessment of Family Exposures (CAFE) Consortium Toolkit. This includes surveys, codebooks, and analysis scripts.

This volume includes documentation for the CAFE Consortium's Media Assessment Questionnaire (MAQ). The MAQ is designed to capture demographic and household characteristics and the amount, type, content, context of media use in families with young children. The MAQ is the result of ongoing collaboration among an international group of CAFE Consortium Key Investigators. The MAQ is supplemented by several other questionnaires including the Macarthur-Bates Communicative Development Inventory (Fenson et al., 2000), the Parent Stress Scale (Berry & Jones, 1995), and the DISRUPT measure of parent distraction with mobile devices (McDaniel, 2021).

vol_name: Searching in the Sand: Protracted Video Deficit in U.S. Preschoolers' Spatial Recall Using a Continuous Search Space
vol_description:
Tasks: Spatial recall task using a continuous search space (sandbox). An A-not-B design enabled measurement of proactive interference on B trials. Children viewed the hiding events either through a window or on a video screen from a live video feed.

Participants: A convenience sample of 56 children (Mage = 4.82 years, SDage = 0.50 years, rangeage = 4.13 to 5.89 years; 19 girls, 37 boys) was recruited for this study. Most parents (84%) identified their child’s race and ethnicity as white and non-Hispanic; the remaining were white and Hispanic (5%), Asian or Asian American (5%), Black or African American (2%), or another race (4%). Parents also reported maternal education, with the majority (88%) reporting at least a 4-year college degree. Five participants were excluded from analyses due to noncompliance (n = 2) or incomplete data (n = 3). The remaining 51 children were assigned to either the screen (n = 25, Mage = 4.81 years, 9 girls, 16 boys) or window (n = 26, Mage = 4.63 years, 10 girls, 16 boys) condition semi-randomly to approximately equate mean age.

File types: .csv coded data for analysis; .csv coded data for inter-rater reliability; text to paste into R markdown file for data processing and analysis; .xlsx codebook describing variables in data file
